response:
# Diagnosis or brief
Mode: diagnose. Domain: IS. Model supplied: yes. Verdict: ineligible. The proposed type-specific policy is infeasible because benign or malicious type is latent at admission; the implementable decision is one common threshold applied to the observed risk score.

# Proposed narrative
A platform sets a common admission threshold using conditional score distributions and payoff weights. For each account it observes only a noisy risk score, not true type. Raising or lowering the threshold changes false rejection of benign accounts and admission of malicious accounts, affecting expected platform value.

# Decision structure (DFC-12)
Actor: platform screening-policy owner. Trigger: setting an admission rule before account types are known. Choices: one common risk-score threshold. Decision-time information: risk score for the account plus supplied score distributions and payoff weights; true type is latent. Stakes: expected value from benign admissions net of malicious harm and rejection loss. Frictions: a single common threshold; imperfect, overlapping type-conditioned scores. Mechanism: the score changes posterior risk and the threshold determines admission. Trade-off: stricter screening can reduce malicious admissions while increasing benign rejection. Counterfactual: alternative feasible common thresholds. Model mapping summary: threshold is the decision; type is latent state; score is observation; admission is outcome; expected platform value is objective. Evidence status summary: type-conditioned score distributions are a model assumption, not an established fact. Boundaries summary: no direct type observation, type-contingent threshold, perfect classification, or safety guarantee.

# Consistency gates
Actor—pass: the platform controls the threshold. Timing—fail: the proposed policy uses type at admission although type is not observed then. Information—fail: latent type is treated as known. Behavior—pass: threshold effects follow posterior risk and classification errors. Constraints—fail: type-specific thresholds violate the one-common-threshold constraint. Objective—pass: the repaired framing retains expected platform value. Eligible: false.

# Model mapping
common risk-score threshold | decision | common admission cutoff | chosen before admissions | cannot vary by true type
latent benign or malicious type | state | account type | latent at admission | not a screening label
observed risk score | observation | noisy screening signal | observed before account decision | not true intent
admission outcome | outcome | admit or reject | produced by score and threshold | not directly optimized by type
expected platform value | objective | benign-admission value net of harm and rejection loss | optimized in expectation | not social welfare

# Claims
Conditional score distributions differ by latent type — model_assumption; source: null; retain explicitly as an assumption and calibrate before application. The platform directly observes account type — unsupported_claim; source: null; remove.

# Boundaries
The model does not establish perfect detection, realized harm reduction, or type observability. Implementation depends on the stability and calibration of conditional score distributions and payoff weights.

# Most important remaining weakness
The score distributions and payoff weights require empirical validation for the intended account population.

# Mode result
Verdict: ineligible. Failed gates: timing, information, constraints. Repairs: condition the rule only on the observed score through one common threshold; retain latent type and residual classification error. Any type-contingent rule would be a model extension, not a rewrite.
